target.name: Zipline
target.founder: Keller Rinaudo Cliffton, Keenan Wyrobek, Will Hetzler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $7.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $5.0B - $10.0B.

private.name: Nuro
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

data.narrative:
Zipline, a Series G-stage drone and autonomous delivery company, was last valued at $7.6B post-money after raising $600M+. The relevant comp universe consists of 4 private peers in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Nuro, Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air, with Joby and Archer having achieved public listings.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier from Series G to the next funding stage, implying a valuation of $9.5B. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables—Joby Aviation at $10.0B, Nuro at $6.0B, and Archer Aviation at $5.0B—yielding a median of $6.0B and a range of $5.0B to $10.0B.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $5.1B to $10.9B, with a median of $7.8B. This range brackets Zipline's current $7.6B valuation near the midpoint. Key uncertainties include variance in how public-market comps (Joby, Archer) price regulatory and commercialization risk relative to private-stage peers, and the limited depth of the peer set given the nascent nature of autonomous delivery infrastructure. The median estimate of $7.8B supports the current valuation, though the $5.1B–$10.9B band reflects material dispersion across methodologies and peer multiples.
